summary: Demand evidence agents completed and PRD readiness passed.

demand_status.summary: Demand is not PRD-ready; missing slots, blockers, assumptions, or evidence remain.

triage.explanation: Fast route by default; no hard factual risk trigger was found.

problem: Existing YOLO harness boundary audit must detect tracked and untracked target-project mutations in git-backed and non-git projects.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
risks: A full-tool agent could create a new target project file without readiness blockers.

blockers.message: explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
explorer must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.
verifier must complete with evidence and a result before cross_check PRD readiness.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

agent_results.claim: The YOLO demand evidence dispatch boundary snapshot system combines git ls-files for tracked/cached/other files with fallback walks, excludes the allowed artifact root, and can detect mutations in both git-backed and non-git projects.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot combines git ls-files output with walkFiles fallback and excludes allowed roots
evidence.why: This is the core boundary audit function that creates file digests for mutation detection.

evidence.summary: gitFiles runs 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z' capturing tracked, cached, and other files
evidence.why: Git ls-files -co covers tracked, cached, and other untracked non-excluded files for git-backed projects.

evidence.summary: walkFiles recursively walks directories excluding .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache
evidence.why: Fallback walk for non-git projects or when git is unavailable, with standard build artifact exclusions.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot combines git files and walkFiles output: [...git, ...walkFiles(projectRoot)]
evidence.why: Merges git-tracked files with walked files for comprehensive coverage in hybrid scenarios.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot excludes allowed roots and creates SHA256 digests for all non-excluded files
evidence.why: Allowed roots (artifact directories) are excluded from mutation detection; file digests enable change detection.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after maps to detect added, modified, and deleted files
evidence.why: This comparison function detects mutations by comparing file digest changes.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime takes boundary snapshots before/after agent execution and detects mutations
evidence.why: Runtime integration that actually performs boundary audits and reports mutations.

evidence.summary: Boundary mutations add BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers to readiness even when agent results pass
evidence.why: Boundary violations block PRD readiness regardless of agent-reported success.

evidence.summary: Test confirms boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass
evidence.why: Test proves boundary audit detects mutations and blocks readiness independently of agent claims.

evidence.summary: Test confirms git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations
evidence.why: Test proves git ls-files + walk combination detects new untracked files in git repos.

evidence.summary: Test confirms git-backed boundary detects gitignored project mutations outside excluded dirs
evidence.why: Test proves boundary detects .gitignored mutations (excluding standard excluded dirs).

evidence.summary: Test confirms non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts
evidence.why: Test proves walkFiles fallback works for non-git projects and respects allowed roots.

evidence.summary: Test confirms boundary mutation probe prompts a controlled write and blocks readiness
evidence.why: Test demonstrates stress-test probe for full-tool agents; mutation is detected and blocked.

evidence.summary: isWithin checks if a path is within a root using relative() to prevent directory traversal
evidence.why: Boundary exclusion logic relies on proper path containment checking.

evidence.summary: excludedDir returns true for .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache
evidence.why: Standard build artifacts are excluded from boundary walks to reduce noise and false positives.

evidence.summary: fileDigest creates SHA256 hash of file contents; returns null for directories or read errors
evidence.why: SHA256 digests enable accurate change detection; error handling prevents crashes on inaccessible files.

risks: Full-tool agent could bypass boundary by not writing files but still causing side effects
Git ls-files -co may behave differently across git versions or unusual git configurations
SHA256 digest computation may be slow for large codebases
WalkFiles fallback may miss files in exotic filesystems or with unusual permissions
Concurrent agent executions could interfere with boundary snapshot consistency

result.notes: Implementation meets acceptance criteria: git ls-files + walkFiles hybrid covers tracked/cached/other files and provides non-git fallback; allowed artifact root exclusion is implemented; comprehensive test coverage confirms detection in git-backed, non-git, untracked, gitignored, and stress-probe scenarios.

agent_results.claim: Verified that the explorer's evidence is accurate: the YOLO demand evidence dispatch boundary snapshot system combines git ls-files for tracked/cached/other files with fallback walks, excludes the allowed artifact root, and can detect mutations in both git-backed and non-git projects, with comprehensive test coverage.

evidence.summary: Verified buildBoundarySnapshot implementation matches explorer claim: combines git ls-files with walkFiles fallback, excludes allowed roots via isWithin check, creates SHA256 digests
evidence.why: Spot-check confirms core boundary audit function implementation is accurate.

evidence.summary: Verified gitFiles runs exact command claimed: 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z' for tracked/cached/other files
evidence.why: Confirms git coverage claim is accurate.

evidence.summary: Verified excludedDir excludes exactly the directories claimed: .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache
evidence.why: Confirms standard build artifact exclusion claim is accurate.

evidence.summary: Verified test confirms boundary blocks mutation (line 522-524) even when agent returns success
evidence.why: Confirms test proves boundary blocks readiness independently of agent claims.

evidence.summary: Verified boundary mutations add BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers (lines 659-664) and are merged into runtime blockers (line 671)
evidence.why: Confirms boundary violation blocking mechanism is implemented as claimed.

risks: Explorer's identified risks remain valid: full-tool agent side-effects beyond file writes, git version/edge cases, large codebase performance, exotic filesystem walk issues, concurrent execution consistency

result.notes: Spot-checked 5 key evidence points across code and tests; all paths, line ranges, and summaries are accurate. Explorer presented no assumptions as facts. All evidence is project-scoped with usable paths. The claim is fully supported by verified implementation and test coverage.
